title: Grace! ’tis a charming sound
textAttribution: Text: Philip Doddridge (1702–1751), Augustus Montague Toplady (1740–1778).
publicDomainBasis: Philip Doddridge (1702–1751), Augustus Montague Toplady (1740–1778), Ira David Sankey (1840–1908). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Grace! ’tis a charming sound,
Harmonious to the ear;
Heav’n with the echo shall resound,
And all the earth shall hear.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
’Twas grace that wrote my name
In life’s eternal book;
’Twas grace that gave me to the Lamb,
Who all my sorrows took.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Grace taught my wandering feet
To tread the pilgrim road;
And new supplies each hour I meet
While pressing on to God.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Grace taught my heart to pray,
And made my eyes o’erflow;
’Tis grace which kept me to this day,
And will not let me go.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Grace all the work shall crown
Through everlasting days;
It lays in love the topmost stone,
And well deserves the praise.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Oh, let that grace inspire
My heart with strength divine;
May all my powers to Thee aspire,
And all my days be Thine.
